subject: mg_regard.ts — C126 fix verification (tieredValue array-of-arrays)

mg_regard_c126_verification.tieredValue_type_notes: tieredValue<T>(values: readonly [T,T,T,T]): T — with [[3,4,5],[4,5,6],[4,5,6],[5,6,7]] as const, T is inferred as each inner readonly tuple. TypeScript resolves roundLengths as readonly [3,4,5]|readonly [4,5,6]|readonly [5,6,7], which satisfies readonly number[]. No unsafe cast, no any. Compile-time safe.

roundLengths_usages.note: Array length — works on any readonly array
Bounds guard — executed before index access
Only reached after guard at line 135; no OOB possible
reduce on readonly number[] — valid, returns totalSymbols
Second guard in render loop before calling endGame()

mg_regard_c126_verification.bounds_check_notes: Double-guarded: prepareRound() at line 135 and render() feedback phase at line 282. currentRound increments only in render() phase='feedback' path. No path can reach roundLengths[currentRound] without passing the guard.
mg_regard_c126_verification.score_calc_notes: endGame() computes: totalSymbols = roundLengths.reduce(sum) [12-18]; finalScore = (correctTotal / totalSymbols) * 100. attemptsTotal is UI-only (mid-game 'Correct: X/Y' display). correctTotal is bounded by totalSymbols since clickedIndex is capped at sequence.length per round. Score is always in [0,100]. No division-by-zero: totalSymbols >= 12 for all tiers.
mg_regard_c126_verification.score_calc_edge_case: Wrong clicks beyond sequence length accumulate attemptsTotal without affecting finalScore. This is intentional — score rewards correct symbol recall, not click economy.

tieredValue_coverage_final.notes: C126 completes mg_regard tieredValue adoption. All 14 minigames now use tieredValue for all difficulty-scaled parameters. First use of array-of-arrays (tuple-of-tuples) in the codebase; TypeScript inference handles it correctly via generic T.

evidence: tieredValue<T> signature is generic — T inferred as inner tuple type, not 'number', preserving readonly tuple literal types
Math.min(difficultyTier, 3) clamps index to [0-3] — no OOB on the outer 4-tuple
All 6 roundLengths usages verified safe — 2 guards before indexing, 1 reduce, 1 length, 2 display
All 4 tier roundLengths are length-3 tuples — roundLengths.length === 3 invariant holds across all tiers
Score denominator is totalSymbols (reduce sum), not attemptsTotal — bounded and non-zero
